page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g7nex.4xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine instance based on the KVM hypervisor. It features 16 dedicated physical cores on an Intel Xeon 8369B processor with an x86_64 architecture, running at a clock speed of 2.7 GHz. The instance is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, meaning storage requirements must be met via network-attached volumes. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 20 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks place this instance in the average tier across multiple metrics. The Intel Xeon processor delivers a stress-ng single-core score of 1858.69 and a multi-core score of 15508.96. Memory performance is characterized by a read-only small block cached bandwidth of 848.41 GB/sec, which drops to 98.36 GB/sec for uncached large blocks, alongside a write bandwidth of 56.31 GB/sec. The system handles data processing tasks with a single-threaded gzip compression rate of 27.71 GB/sec and a multi-threaded bzip3 decompression rate of 82.89 GB/sec. Database operations are supported by a Redis SET performance of 15.47 million operations per second and a static web serving throughput of 20.58 GB/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the dedicated core allocation and balanced memory-to-vCPU ratio offer predictable performance for standard enterprise workloads without the overhead of specialized accelerator hardware. The absence of local storage requires architectural consideration for I/O-heavy applications, making it best suited for workloads reliant on network-attached storage. This instance is appropriate for general-purpose applications, including web serving, database operations, and moderate data processing tasks.
